text: Mitochondrial fatty acid β-oxidation is an important biochemical mechanism that is vital in maintaining energy homeostasis in the liver (Houten and Wanders 2010; Naguib et al. 2019).  It is important in whole organism energy production during fasting but also serves as the main mechanism for fatty acid degradation and removal (Houten and Wanders 2010; Cherkaoui-Malki et al. 2012; Naguib et al. 2019).  When fatty acid β-oxidation is decreased in the liver, lipids are not able to be eliminated as efficiently and can to begin to accumulate in the liver (Fromenty 2019; He et al. 2019; Naguib et al. 2019). Therefore, a decrease in or complete inhibition of mitochondrial fatty acid β-oxidation can result in disrupted lipid storage in the liver.
